_README: THESE DEFAULTS PUT EVERYTHING IN A 'data' FOLDER BESIDE THE APP, so a fresh clone runs with no
setup at all. Point DbPath at your real database when you want the real database.
Relative paths resolve against the app's content root, so 'data/timesheet.db' means
<this folder>/data/timesheet.db. Absolute paths (C:\... or \\server\share\...) are used as-is.
DbPath: an existing database at this path is OPENED as-is, never replaced. Nothing there yet ->
a fresh one is created and seeded with an 'admin' / 'admin' account. Change that password
immediately - there is a Change Password screen in the sidebar under ACCOUNT.
ConfigPath: the WRITABLE settings store (backup folder, retention window, export roots). It does
not have to exist yet - it is created on first write. It must NOT be this file.
KeyRingPath: Data Protection keys. These must OUTLIVE the process - delete them and every signed-in
user is logged out. If you point DbPath somewhere real, point this somewhere backed up too.
Any of these can also be set as an environment variable, e.g. TimesheetApp__DbPath, which wins over
this file. The startup banner prints the paths that ACTUALLY won, every start. Read it - since M11,
editing this file changes which database opens, and that is exactly how you open the wrong one.
Deleting a key entirely is not the same as leaving the default: the app REFUSES TO START and names
the missing key. That is deliberate. A missing location used to fall through silently to the desktop
app's %APPDATA% paths, which named the live company database.

TimesheetApp._SeedFirstAdmin: Optional, default true. Set false ONLY if you provision accounts out of band - a fresh database with it off cannot be logged into by anyone.
